sections.paragraph:
ὑπερευφραίνομαι (Arrian, Cyneg. 7, 2; Lucian, Icar. 2; Ps.-Lucian, Amor. 5; AcPlTh 7 [Aa I 241, 1]) rejoice exceedingly ἐπί τινι (Jos., Bell. 7, 14) B 1:2.—DELG s.v. φρήν.

Arrian Arrian = Flavius Arrianus, I–II a.d.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Ps.-Lucian Ps.-Lucian , after II a.d.—List 5

AcPlTh AcPlTh = Acts of Paul and Thecla—List 5

Aa Aa = Acta apostolorum apocrypha—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1033
